notes: VERIFIED PER-SET SOURCE (ThePriceDex sm11, re-fetched live): per-pack odds Rare non-holo 1/1.5=0.66667; Rare Holo 1/7.2=0.13889; Rare Holo GX 1/7.8=0.12821; Ultra Rare (full-art) 1/22.4=0.04464; Rainbow Rare 1/76.9=0.01300; Secret/Gold Rare 1/111.1=0.00900. These six per-pack probabilities sum to ~0.9985 (rounding), confirming a single rare-or-better slot model. COARSE-BUCKET MAPPING onto engine buckets (SM era lumps all GX + full-art Pokemon + full-art Trainers => ultra-rare; all rainbow + gold + secret => secret-rare; holo + non-holo rares => rare): ultra-rare = P(Rare Holo GX)+P(Ultra Rare full-art) = 0.12821+0.04464 = 0.17285; secret-rare = P(Rainbow)+P(Secret gold) = 0.01300+0.00900 = 0.02200; rare = remainder (Rare non-holo + Rare Holo) = 1 - 0.17285 - 0.02200 = 0.80515 (matches P(Rare non-holo)+P(Rare Holo)=0.66667+0.13889=0.80556 within rounding). Slot sums to EXACTLY 1.0. Keys {rare, ultra-rare, secret-rare} all in this set's AVAILABLE buckets, which I INDEPENDENTLY RECOMPUTED from the project's local card data (data/snapshot/snapshot.json, sm11): common 65, uncommon 72, rare 59, ultra-rare 40, secret-rare 22 (258 cards total) — exactly matching the stated buckets. BOX-LEVEL SANITY vs ThePriceDex per-box: ultra-rare 0.17285*36 = 6.22/box (ThePriceDex 4.6 GX + 1.6 Ultra Rare = 6.2 box-avg, matches); secret-rare 0.02200*36 = 0.79/box (ThePriceDex 0.5 rainbow + 0.3 secret = 0.8, matches). Hit economy is sane (not a GX every pack). STRUCTURE: ThePriceDex lists 11 cards/pack (10 value cards + 1 basic Energy); modeled as 10 value cards = 5 commons, 3 uncommons, 1 reverse-holo (50/50 common/uncommon filler, no hit value), 1 rare-or-better EV slot; basic Energy not modeled. packSize=10 per era convention (matches sibling crimson-invasion.json). PRODUCTS: standard 36-pack Booster Display Box confirmed via pokemontcg.com homepage ('36 booster packs, each 10 cards + 1 basic Energy') and the project's own snapshot data which lists a 'display' sealed product for sm11; ETB = 8 packs confirmed via CollectorsCache ('8 booster packs') and Amazon listing. Standard set -> shipEv true.
